name: the-48-laws-of-power
description: Robert Greene's masterwork on power dynamics — strategy distilled from 3,000 years of history. Covers: workplace power plays, social dynamics, negotiation tactics, self-defense against manipulation, and leadership strategy.
useCase: Navigate power dynamics in any social or professional setting — understand how power is gained, protected, and lost.
knownRisks: These laws are descriptive of how power works, not prescriptive for every situation. Using them without ethical consideration can damage relationships.
Some laws contradict each other — context determines which law applies.
Over-application can make you appear manipulative and erode trust.
